content:
{
  "title": "Type-Aware Prompting for Enhanced Code Generation with Large Language Models",
  "problem": "Existing prompting strategies for LLM-based code generation do not explicitly leverage fine-grained type information inherent in programming problems, often leading to syntactic or semantic errors. This work targets improving code correctness and adherence to type constraints via prompting.",
  "motivation": "Type systems are central to modern programming languages and serve as critical sources of structure and error prevention. While current LLMs can generate code, they frequently violate type constraints or produce brittle code. Integrating type-awareness into the prompting process could substantially increase the reliability and practical utility of LLM-generated code.",
  "method": "We introduce a novel 'Type-Aware Prompting' (TAP) framework, where prompts are automatically augmented with explicit type annotations and required type signatures extracted from the input specification or inferred from context. Given a natural language problem statement, the TAP engine parses or infers the involved types (e.g., function signatures, variable types, expected return types) and appends a structured type summary to the prompt alongside the task instructions. The LLM is thereby guided to generate code that adheres strictly to these type constraints, reducing type errors and improving function correctness. The framework supports both statically-typed (e.g., Java, C++) and dynamically-typed (e.g., Python) languages by providing canonical type hints or pseudo-signatures.",
  "experiment_plan": "We will evaluate TAP on HumanEval and MBPP code generation benchmarks, including both statically and dynamically-typed language tasks. Baselines will include standard CoT prompting, Structured CoT, and self-revision methods. Metrics will comprise pass@1, pass@10, and syntactic/type error rates, with ablations to assess the contribution of type-aware augmentation."
}
